share.title: Share Torah Roll Helper
share.copyFailed: Could not copy. Select the text manually.
share.openFailed: Could not open the share target.
share.nativeFailed: Sharing failed. Try another option below.
share.message:
📜 Before every Torah reading, your Gabbai quietly solves a problem most people never see: which column is the scroll open to? How many to roll to reach the next reading? Which Sefer Torah in the Aron is closest?

There's a free tool that handles all of this in seconds.

Torah Rolling Assistant identifies the exact column from a photo of the Klaf or the first words visible on the page — then tells you precisely how many columns to roll and in which direction, with every Torah reading that can occur throughout the year already built in.

No account. No installation. Works on any phone. 100% free.

👉 {link}

Please share this in your Shul's WhatsApp group. Most community members belong to several synagogues — one share reaches far more Gabbaim than you'd expect. If you're a Gabbai yourself, pass it along to the Gabbaim you know.
share.messageShort:
📜 Free tool for Gabbaim — identify which column a Torah scroll is open to (camera or page's first words), then get the exact rolling direction and column count. All Shabbat, Yom Tov, and special readings built in. No account, no installation.

👉 {link}

seo.fallbackTitle: Torah Roll Helper | Assistant de roulement de la Torah | עוזר גלילת התורה
seo.fallbackDescription: Free Torah scroll rolling assistant for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah. Calculate the direction and number of columns to roll between Torah readings. Also known as Assistant de roulement de la Torah and עוזר גלילת התורה.

home.title: Torah Roll Helper | Free Sefer Torah rolling assistant
home.description: Free tool for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah to calculate how many Torah columns to roll and in which direction between readings.

about.title: About | Torah Roll Helper
about.description: Learn what Torah Roll Helper does, where its Torah reading data comes from, and how to contact or contribute to the project.

howTo.title: How To Use | Torah Roll Helper
howTo.description: Learn how to use Torah Roll Helper to identify the current Torah position, choose the next reading, and get clear rolling instructions.

about.title: About
about.description: Built for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah to reduce Torach Tzibbur. Set the current column and the target reading — the app instantly calculates the rolling direction and exact Amud count.
about.dataCreditsPrefix: The database for this project is based on open-source files from

about.dataCreditsThanks: Our thanks go to the tikkun.io project for sharing their work with the community.
about.issueInvite: If you have an improvement idea or wish to add a language, please open an issue on GitHub.
about.emailInvite: For direct contact, you are welcome to reach us by email.

instructions.1: Fit the line width inside the white frame.
instructions.2: Align the text using the yellow lines.
instructions.3: Capture at least four lines of text.
instructions.4: Ensure the first line is full (no large blanks).

dicta.chooseSubtitle: Multiple matches were found. Select the correct page.

golaBanner.text: Israel reading schedule active — are you outside Israel?

howTo.title: How To Use
howTo.subtitle: Follow these quick steps to get clear rolling instructions.

steps.step1: Identify the current location of the Torah scroll (the parasha it is currently open on) using the camera, exact manual input, or the page's first words.
steps.step2: Select your target reading from the upcoming list or the full index.
steps.step3: The app calculates the rolling direction (Forward/Backward) and the number of columns to roll.

howTo.tip: Tip: Set FROM first, then pick TO.

camera.title: Camera usage
camera.step1: Allow camera access when prompted by your browser.
camera.step2: Place the Torah text inside the frame in good, even lighting.
camera.step3: Hold steady for a moment so detection can lock correctly.
camera.step4: If detection fails, use the page's first words or manual input to set the current location.

tutorials.title: Interactive screen tours
tutorials.subtitle: These tours guide you through the actual app interface. Use the short tour for the basic workflow, or the full tour to master every tool.

tutorialPrompt.title: New here?
tutorialPrompt.text: Guided tours are available on the 'How To' page. Start a quick tour right now to learn the basics.

step1.title: Language selection
step1.description: Click or tap here to change the app's language. The tour will pause so you can open the menu.

step2.title: Choose your language
step2.description: Select your preferred language. Tutorial text will update immediately. If current language is good, tap Next.

step3.title: Set the current scroll location
step3.description: This is the FROM side. Use 'Choose reading' for all readings, 'First line' to identify the open column using the page's first words, 'Take photo' to identify the open column from the Klaf, or 'Enter ref.' for an exact reference or page number. The cards below show last month's readings for quick access.

step4.title: Choose your destination
step4.description: This is the TO side. This calendar strip shows upcoming readings. Most of the time, you will simply click the next Parasha here. We've selected an example for you.

step5.title: Your rolling instructions
step5.description: This card provides your result: the number of Amudim (columns) to move, the rolling direction, and the exact FROM and TO pages used for the calculation.

step1.title: Language selection
step1.description: Click or tap here to change the app's language. The tour will pause so you can open the menu.

step2.title: Choose your language
step2.description: Select your preferred language. Tutorial text will update immediately. If current language is good, tap Next.

step3.title: The FROM card
step3.description: Use this section to define where the Sefer Torah is currently open. The top buttons offer three ways to find a reading, while the cards below show last month's readings for a quick selection.

step4.title: The full reading list
step4.description: Click 'Choose reading' when you need to access the complete list of readings.

step5.title: The index overlay
step5.description: Use the search bar at the top, or expand the categories below to find the exact Kriah you need.

step6.title: Search by first line
step6.description: Click 'First line' to identify your current page by typing the first words that appear at the top of the column.

step7.title: Search by first words
step7.description: Start typing the first words that appear at the top of the page. Each result shows the line that opens that page. If needed, open the built-in Hebrew keyboard, use the camera to scan the first line, or switch to match anywhere inside that line.

step8.title: Page recognition (Camera)
step8.description: Click the camera icon when the Sefer Torah is open and you want the app to identify your location directly from the text on the Klaf.

step9.title: Camera results
step9.description: After taking a photo, the app scans the text and suggests the most likely Amudim for your current location. If the photo misses, the same screen now offers a direct fallback to typing the page's first words.

step10.title: Manual input
step10.description: Click 'Enter ref.' when you already know the exact Perek/Pasuk or the specific page number in the Tikkun Korim.

step11.title: Exact location dialog
step11.description: Here you can set the Sefer, Perek, and Pasuk, or jump straight to a page number.

step12.title: The TO card
step12.description: This is where you set your destination. The calendar strip displays the upcoming month. In most cases, you will simply select the next reading from here.

step13.title: Reference point
step13.description: Choose your exact stopping point. You can target the start of the Parasha, the end of a weekday reading (Sheni/Hamishi/Mincha), or the end of the full Shabbat reading.

step14.title: The result card
step14.description: Once FROM and TO are set, this card gives you the final answer: how many Amudim to roll, which direction, and the reference pages.

step15.title: Crossing into a new Chumash
step15.description: If your roll crosses into a new book (e.g., from Exodus to Leviticus), this note tells you how many pages are left to roll after reaching target book.

step16.title: Page preview
step16.description: Click the page number or the small book icon to see a preview of the selected column.

step17.title: Verify with the Tikkun
step17.description: The preview shows the first line of the column (with an option to toggle Nikud) and includes a button to open a full digital Tikkun layout to verify your spot.

step18.title: Settings
step18.description: Click the cog icon to open your preferences.

step19.title: Community customs
step19.description: Use settings to toggle between Israel and Diaspora (Gola) modes, select your Nusach, and choose the scroll layout (e.g., 245 columns). These options affect which readings appear and how the roll is calculated.

step20.title: Navigation
step20.description: Use the navigation bar to access other tools. On mobile, tap the menu icon to open the sidebar.

step21.title: About & Contact
step21.description: When you finish this tour, the 'About' page will open so you can see project details, view data credits, or report an issue on GitHub.

help.gola: If you are outside Israel, enable Gola mode to synchronize readings with the Diaspora calendar.
help.nusach: Choose your reading tradition. Right now, only Sefaradic is available.
help.torahType: Most sifrei Torah use the רמ"ה layout (245 pages). Other layouts exist, like the Teimani (תאג') which is 226 pages.

savedSettingsBanner.defaultText: Settings are saved in this browser.

manual.title: Set Location Manually
manual.page_not_found: Page not found for the selected reference.

firstLineSearch.title: Search by First Words
firstLineSearch.description: Type the first words at the top of the column. Each result shows the opening line of that column.
firstLineSearch.ocrCameraInstruction1: Fit only the first line inside the white frame.
firstLineSearch.ocrCameraInstruction2: Align the line with the yellow guides.
firstLineSearch.ocrCameraInstruction3: Keep the letters large, sharp, and well lit.
firstLineSearch.ocrCameraInstruction4: Avoid extra lines above or below the frame.
firstLineSearch.ocrPanelHint: Take a tight photo of the first line. The corrected text is inserted in the field above so you can refine it.
firstLineSearch.ocrEditableText: Corrected text in the search field
firstLineSearch.ocrNoTextDetected: No usable Hebrew text was detected in that photo.
firstLineSearch.ocrUnexpectedError: Could not run OCR on this image. Try another photo.
firstLineSearch.ocrLowQuality: Image too blurry. Try a tighter shot of the first line, or type manually.
firstLineSearch.emptyState: Find the column by its opening words
firstLineSearch.emptyStateHint: Type the first words of the opening line.
firstLineSearch.keepTyping: Type a bit more to narrow the results.
firstLineSearch.layoutNote: Based on scroll layout in Settings (current: {layout})
firstLineSearch.noMatchesHint: Try fewer words, or switch to searching anywhere in the opening line.
firstLineSearch.noMatchesLayout: Make sure the scroll layout in Settings matches your Sefer Torah.
firstLineSearch.noMatchesTryAnywhere: Search anywhere in the line instead
firstLineSearch.searchAnywhere: Search anywhere in the opening line
